title: Housing Benefit (pension age)
jurisdiction: England, Wales and Scotland (pension-age claims; working-age rent help is Universal Credit)

effective.uprating: The Social Security Benefits Up-rating Order 2026 (S.I. 2026/148)

plainly: Help with rent for people over State Pension age. The passport that matters: on Pension Credit (Guarantee Credit), income and savings are disregarded entirely and Housing Benefit is normally your full eligible rent.

questions.label: Your date of birth
questions.help: Only used to work out whether you have reached State Pension age — it never leaves this page.
questions.rule: Housing Benefit (SPC) Regulations 2006 (S.I. 2006/214) reg 5 (these are the pension-age rules); under State Pension age, rent help is Universal Credit

questions.label: Do you live with a partner (married, civil partner, or as a couple)?
questions.rule: Housing Benefit (SPC) Regulations 2006: a couple's income, capital and applicable amount are assessed together

questions.label: Your partner's date of birth
questions.rule: New pension-age Housing Benefit claims need both partners over State Pension age (since 15 May 2019; S.I. 2019/37)

questions.label: If one of you is under State Pension age: do you get pension-age Housing Benefit as a couple?
questions.help: Couples already claiming before 15 May 2019 keep transitional protection. If unsure, answer no.
questions.rule: Welfare Reform Act 2012 (Commencement No. 31 etc.) Order 2019 (S.I. 2019/37), savings provisions; gov.uk Housing Benefit eligibility

questions.label: Do you pay rent for the home you live in?
questions.help: Housing Benefit is for rent. Homeowners with a mortgage cannot get it — the equivalent is Support for Mortgage Interest, a different scheme.
questions.rule: Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992 s.130(1)(a) (liability to make payments in respect of a dwelling occupied as the home)

questions.label: Your weekly eligible rent
questions.help: Social tenants: usually your actual rent. Private tenants: capped at your area's Local Housing Allowance rate, which this page cannot know — look yours up at lha-direct.voa.gov.uk and enter the lower of the two. Some service charges are not eligible.
questions.rule: Housing Benefit (SPC) Regulations 2006 reg 50 (maximum housing benefit starts from the eligible rent)

questions.label: Do you — or a partner you live with — get Pension Credit?
questions.help: If the Pension Credit answer above says you appear eligible, claim that first: on Guarantee Credit your income and savings are disregarded here entirely.
questions.rule: Housing Benefit (SPC) Regulations 2006 reg 26 (claimant in receipt of guarantee credit: whole income and capital disregarded)

questions.label: Your weekly State Pension (and your partner's, added together)
questions.rule: Housing Benefit (SPC) Regulations 2006 reg 29 (meaning of income: retirement pension income)

questions.label: Weekly income from workplace or private pensions and annuities (both of you)
questions.rule: Housing Benefit (SPC) Regulations 2006 reg 29 (meaning of income)

questions.label: Weekly earnings from work, after tax (both of you)
questions.help: Enter 0 if none. A small amount is disregarded — the working-out below shows it.
questions.rule: Housing Benefit (SPC) Regulations 2006 reg 29; Sch 4 (sums disregarded from earnings)

questions.label: Other weekly income that counts — for example Carer's Allowance or Working Tax Credit (both of you)
questions.help: Do NOT include Attendance Allowance, DLA, PIP, Pension Age Disability Payment or Housing Benefit itself. Enter 0 if none.
questions.rule: Housing Benefit (SPC) Regulations 2006 reg 29 (which income counts)

questions.label: Total savings and investments (both of you) — not your home
questions.help: Over £16,000 normally ends entitlement — unless you get Guarantee Credit, when savings are disregarded entirely.
questions.rule: Housing Benefit (SPC) Regulations 2006 reg 43 (capital limit); reg 29(2) (deemed income from capital)

questions.label: Do you get Attendance Allowance, the daily-living part of PIP or ADP, the middle or higher care rate of DLA, or Pension Age Disability Payment?
questions.rule: Housing Benefit (SPC) Regulations 2006 Sch 3 para 6 (severe disability premium)

questions.label: Does your partner get one of those benefits too?
questions.rule: Housing Benefit (SPC) Regulations 2006 Sch 3 para 6 (a couple's severe disability premium needs both to qualify)

questions.label: Apart from your partner, do any other adults (18 or over) normally live with you?
questions.rule: Housing Benefit (SPC) Regulations 2006 reg 55 (non-dependant deductions) and Sch 3 para 6 (severe disability premium)

questions.label: Does anyone get Carer's Allowance (or Scotland's carer support payment) for looking after you or your partner?
questions.rule: Housing Benefit (SPC) Regulations 2006 Sch 3 para 6 (no severe disability premium while a carer is paid for the care)

questions.label: Are you entitled to Carer's Allowance (or carer support payment) for looking after someone else?
questions.help: Answer yes even if it is not actually paid because of your State Pension (an “underlying entitlement” still counts).
questions.rule: Housing Benefit (SPC) Regulations 2006 Sch 3 para 9 (carer premium)

questions.label: Is your partner entitled to Carer's Allowance (or carer support payment)?
questions.rule: Housing Benefit (SPC) Regulations 2006 Sch 3 para 9 (carer premium, each carer)

values.plainly: Your applicable amount: the personal allowance (2026–27: £238.00 single, £363.25 couple) plus any premiums — the same shapes as Pension Credit's minimum guarantee.
values.rule: Housing Benefit (SPC) Regulations 2006 reg 22, Sch 3 Pt 1 para 1 and Pt 4 (as uprated 1 April 2026 by S.I. 2026/148)

values.plainly: Earnings ignored each week: £20 where a carer premium applies, otherwise £10 for a couple, £5 single.
values.rule: Housing Benefit (SPC) Regulations 2006 Sch 4 (sums disregarded from earnings)

values.plainly: Deemed weekly income from savings above £10,000: £1 per £500 or part.
Income that counts — or nothing at all on Guarantee Credit, where the whole of your income and capital is disregarded.
values.rule: Housing Benefit (SPC) Regulations 2006 reg 26 (on guarantee credit); reg 29 otherwise

values.plainly: Income above your applicable amount (nil on Guarantee Credit).
The taper: 65p of Housing Benefit is withdrawn for each £1 of excess income (rounded up to the next penny here — up to 1p a week conservative).
Housing Benefit: your eligible rent, less the taper.

gates.because: These are the pension-age Housing Benefit rules and you have not yet reached State Pension age. Rent help under it is Universal Credit's housing element.
gates.rule: Housing Benefit (SPC) Regulations 2006 reg 5; gov.uk Housing Benefit eligibility

gates.because: A couple cannot start a pension-age Housing Benefit claim while one partner is under State Pension age (couples claiming since before 15 May 2019 keep protection). Until then, rent help is Universal Credit.
Housing Benefit is help with rent. Homeowners cannot get it — help with mortgage interest is a separate scheme (Support for Mortgage Interest).
gates.rule: Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992 s.130(1)(a)

gates.because: Savings over £16,000 end entitlement — unless you get Guarantee Credit, when savings are disregarded entirely. If the Pension Credit answer above applies, claim it first and this gate disappears.

verdicts.because: On Pension Credit your income and savings are disregarded entirely: maximum Housing Benefit, normally your full eligible rent. Claim through your council.

On Pension Credit you get maximum Housing Benefit — but a deduction applies for each other adult living with you, sized by their income (not encoded here), so the award will be below your full rent. Claim through your council.

Your income is above your applicable amount, but 65p per £1 of the excess still leaves some of your rent covered. And if the Pension Credit answer above applies, claim it first — it would make this maximum.

Some rent appears covered after the 65% taper — but a deduction applies for each other adult living with you, sized by their income (not encoded here). Claim through your council and expect the award below the working-out shown.

65p of each £1 of income above your applicable amount exceeds your eligible rent, so the taper reaches zero. Close cases are worth claiming anyway — and a Pension Credit award of even pennies would flip this to maximum Housing Benefit.

not_encoded: non-dependant deductions (reg 55: a deduction per other adult in the home, sized by their income — where they apply, the flagged award will be lower)
Local Housing Allowance caps for private tenants (the eligible rent may be below the contractual rent; look up your area at lha-direct.voa.gov.uk)
ineligible service charges (heating, meals and some others come out of the eligible rent)
the Savings-Credit-only case (reg 27: a partial disregard — this file's Guarantee Credit passport assumes Guarantee Credit)
temporary absence, two homes, and care-home rules
Discretionary Housing Payments (a council top-up worth asking about when Housing Benefit falls short)

passports.name: Maximum Council Tax Reduction usually travels with it
passports.note: claimed through the same council form in most areas.

sources_checked.what: Personal allowances £238.00 / £363.25; severe disability premium £86.05 / £172.10; carer premium £48.15 (uprated 1 April 2026 by S.I. 2026/148)
sources_checked.where: HB (SPC) Regulations 2006 Sch 3 Pts 1 and 4 (legislation.gov.uk revised text)

sources_checked.what: On guarantee credit: whole income and capital disregarded (reg 26); maximum HB (reg 50); 65% taper (reg 51); £16,000 capital limit (reg 43); tariff £1/£500 over £10,000 (reg 29(2))
sources_checked.where: HB (SPC) Regulations 2006 (legislation.gov.uk contents and revised text)

sources_checked.what: New-claim conditions: pension age, mixed-age exclusion with pre-2019 protection, £16,000 limit with Guarantee Credit exception, owner-occupiers excluded (SMI instead)
